title: Legacy001 feature-block ablation
summary: Run a narrow ablation patch against legacy-001 feature engineering to test whether one existing feature block is hurting the RealMLP baseline.
rationale: The parent uses floor-derived categorical features, count encoding, aggressive RaceProgress binning, and target encoding. Some blocks may add noise or overfit, so removing one block at a time can identify which parts actually carry the strong score.
implementation_hint: Patch the parent by disabling exactly one existing feature block, such as numeric floor-categoricals, count encoding, RaceProgress=200 binning, or target encoding. Do not remove multiple blocks, change model parameters, alter folds, add new features, or rewrite the pipeline.
expected_effect: Reveal whether a current feature block is harmful or redundant; a positive result would simplify the baseline and improve future patch reliability.
risk: The ablation may reduce score; its value is diagnostic, and it is meaningful only relative to the legacy-001 parent.
